task: P3-2 measurement spike — empirical fixture-timing for B3 truncation tightening (BACKLOG.md:17, gated by ADR-2)
scope: Read-only artifact analysis (no live smoke re-run per HANDOFF Codex-hang practice). Inputs: tests/skills/.artifacts/{8 fixtures}/meta.json + tool-use-events.json snapshot from 2026-04-30 (plugin v2.6.19). Output: per-fixture truncation profile + recommended approach mapping among ADR-2 options (i)/(ii)/(iii), to feed the next review-loop:plan cycle that closes P3-2.
data_freshness_caveat: Artifact snapshot is 8 days old (plugin v2.6.19; current main is v2.6.31). Six deliveries shipped since (v2.6.20 through v2.6.31) and may have shifted the Read-flurry budget. Plan-locked decision should re-validate the empirical-bump approach (ii) timing against a fresh single-fixture run before committing to a bump value. Rest of the analysis (truncation_kind classification, prompt sizes, last_event positions) is structurally invariant — fixture defs and SKILL doc-recon shape have not changed at the level this analysis depends on.

results.label: fixture-truncation-table
results.description: Per-fixture timing/truncation profile across 8 tool_use_events-capturing smoke fixtures. plan.fresh is the only fixture reaching Agent dispatch within 120s; the other 7 truncate during SKILL doc-recon (Reads against plugin-cache protocol/agents .md files or the seeded session file). Returncode -15 = SIGTERM at 120s wall-clock cap.

plan.fresh.smoke.claude.verdict: REACHES_AGENT_BUT_TIMES_OUT — only fixture passing B3 min:1; succeeds because plan target's doc-recon is shorter than execute target's

execute.from-plan.smoke.claude.verdict: TRUNCATED_IN_DOC_RECON — still in agent-protocol Reads after 120s

execute.review-only.smoke.claude.verdict: TRUNCATED_IN_DOC_RECON — fewer events than from-plan (5 vs 7); still pre-Agent

execute.session-resume.smoke.claude.verdict: TRUNCATED_NEAR_DISPATCH — got past doc-recon to seeded-session Read, but 120s exhausted before Agent invocation

execute.stop-after-before-polish.smoke.c.verdict: TRUNCATED_IN_DOC_RECON_DEEP — most Reads (11) of any truncating fixture but still pre-Agent; suggests this prompt drives broader doc-recon than the others

execute.stop-after-before-security.smoke.verdict: TRUNCATED_NEAR_DISPATCH — past doc-recon to session file Read

execute.stop-after-polish.smoke.claude.verdict: TRUNCATED_NEAR_DISPATCH — past doc-recon to session file Read (twin of stop-after-before-security)

review-loop.regression.smoke.claude.verdict: TRUNCATED_IN_DOC_RECON — entry-point review-loop has highest doc-recon ceiling (must read planning.md, executor-output.md, reviewer-output.md AND polish + delivery agent docs); never even creates session-path.txt

results.how_computed: Read tests/skills/.artifacts/<fixture>/meta.json + tool-use-events.json for each of 8 fixtures and tabulated rc, last event, and event count. Cross-referenced tests/skills/smoke/<fixture>.smoke.claude.json setup blocks to extract claude_prompt_chars (handling both direct-claude and bash-preseed wrapping shapes; latter inlines `claude -p ... -- "<prompt>"` inside a bash -lc body via shell setup that preseeds .review-loop/sessions/<UUID>.md from tests/skills/fixtures/sessions/v2.6.0-approved-session.md).
results.valid_for: Plugin v2.6.19 artifact snapshot. Recommended re-validation: rerun a single fixture (suggest execute.session-resume — shortest preseed prompt, 6 events, near-dispatch profile) at v2.6.31 with timeout bumped to 240s to confirm Agent dispatch is reachable within that bumped envelope before plan-locking approach (ii).

results.label: primary-finding-bottleneck
results.description: Bottleneck is SKILL.md / docs/protocol Read flurry, NOT user prompt size. plan.fresh has the LARGEST prompt (1089 chars) and is the ONLY fixture reaching Agent dispatch. The other 7 prompts span 302-590 chars and all truncate. Therefore approach (i) 'trim the user prompt' has limited remaining leverage — the bash-preseed prompts are already at 300-600 chars and contain mostly task-specifying boilerplate that cannot be trimmed without losing assertion fidelity. The doc-recon Reads (5-11 per fixture into plugin-cache protocol/agents .md files) consume most of the 120s budget regardless of prompt size.

evidence: plan.fresh prompt 1089 chars → reaches Agent in 11 events
all 7 truncating fixtures: prompt 302-590 chars → 5-11 events, all pre-Agent
6 of 7 truncating fixtures last-event is Reading a plugin-cache .md (agents/reviewer.md, docs/protocol/reviewer-output.md) or a seeded session file — all SKILL-internal Reads, not user-driven content
non_evidence: no correlation between prompt size and event count
no correlation between wrap_kind (direct vs bash-preseed) and truncation distance
no correlation between assertion-set size and event count

results.how_computed: Pearson-style inspection: smallest-prompt fixture (302) and largest-prompt fixture (1089) have similar event counts (6 vs 11); largest-prompt fixture is the ONLY success. plan.fresh's success vs all-execute failures is consistent with plan target having a smaller protocol surface (planning.md + executor-output.md + reviewer-output.md + plan/agents/executor.md = 4 mandatory protocol files at SKILL.md entry; execute target adds quality-polish + delivery docs).
results.valid_for: Stable across plugin versions because the SKILL.md doc-recon import surface evolves slowly; spot-checked against current skills/plan and skills/execute headers.

results.label: approach-mapping-per-fixture
results.description: Per-fixture recommended approach among ADR-2 follow-up options (i) trim prompt, (ii) bump timeout, (iii) Agent-only smoke. Recommendations are inputs to plan-locked decision, NOT decisions themselves.

execute.session-resume.smoke.claude.rationale: TRUNCATED_NEAR_DISPATCH — already at seeded-session Read in 6 events; modest bump likely sufficient; preserves end-to-end smoke signal value

execute.stop-after-before-security.smoke.rationale: TRUNCATED_NEAR_DISPATCH — same profile as session-resume; 9 events past doc-recon

execute.from-plan.smoke.claude.rationale: TRUNCATED_IN_DOC_RECON — only 7 events in 120s, last in agents/reviewer.md; needs ~2x budget to reach Agent OR replace with deterministic Agent-only proof

execute.review-only.smoke.claude.rationale: TRUNCATED_IN_DOC_RECON — only 5 events in 120s; same profile as from-plan

execute.stop-after-before-polish.smoke.c.rationale: TRUNCATED_IN_DOC_RECON_DEEP — 11 events but still pre-Agent; this fixture's prompt drives broader doc-recon (--stop-after before-polish requires understanding polish boundary). Bump leaves end-to-end signal intact.

review-loop.regression.smoke.claude.rationale: Most ambitious fixture: full review-loop end-to-end (exec→polish→docs→security). Asserts session-final.md, completed_stages_ordered_exec_polish_docs_security, delivery_blocked_by_null. Even 240s likely insufficient. Either (ii) substantial bump (480s+) or scope-reduction pass on the assertion set in a follow-up. (iii) Agent-only is NOT applicable here — the fixture's purpose is end-to-end review-loop validation.

value.global_alternative: (iii) replace ALL 6 execute fixtures' prompts with deterministic Agent-only smoke (e.g., 'Use the Agent tool with subagent_type general-purpose to write OK to a file'). Decouples B3 assertion from skill-internal doc-recon entirely. Tradeoff: loses end-to-end skill behavior signal — these fixtures would no longer prove that review-loop:execute actually entered a real round. Also leaves the underlying SKILL doc-recon overhead unaddressed (a separate optimization concern that surfaces in CI cold-start scenarios). Suggested as fallback if approach (ii) bumps push CI wall-clock past acceptable threshold.

results.how_computed: Cross-tabulated truncation_kind from fixture-truncation-table against ADR-2 options. (i) trim-prompt rejected for all 7 because user-prompt size is not the bottleneck (see primary-finding-bottleneck). (ii) bump-timeout sized empirically: NEAR_DISPATCH fixtures (~6-9 events past doc-recon) sized at 1.5x = 180s; IN_DOC_RECON fixtures (~5-11 events still in protocol Reads) sized at 2x = 240s; review-loop.regression sized at 4x = 480s due to its full-pipeline assertion set. (iii) Agent-only proposed only as global fallback because per-fixture (iii) loses fixture-specific signal value (e.g., session-resume's preseed contract proof; from-plan's --plan body injection proof).
results.valid_for: Suggested timeout bump values are an empirical first guess derived from event-rate (~1 Read every 10-15s observed; Agent typically tool-call #11 in plan.fresh, would be ~15+ for execute). Plan-locked decision must re-measure on v2.6.31 before committing to a specific bump number. ADR-2 noted 'each fixture truncates for fixture-specific reasons' — that judgment still applies; uniform bump across all 7 is NOT recommended.

results.label: ci-wall-clock-impact
results.description: Worst-case wall-clock impact of approach (ii) per-fixture bumps assuming all 7 fixtures hit their bumped cap (current behavior is timeout-on-all-7 for 120s each = 14 min). Sum across 7 fixtures.

value.interpretation: Approach (ii) roughly doubles the worst-case smoke wall-clock from 14 min to 29 min. Best-case (all fixtures finish before timeout) would be substantially shorter — probably 60-90s/fixture for NEAR_DISPATCH set, 120-180s for IN_DOC_RECON set. The 29-min figure is the upper bound, not the expected case.

results.how_computed: Sum of recommended bumps from approach-mapping-per-fixture. Worst-case == every fixture hits its full timeout (matching current 100% timeout-rate for the 7 fixtures).
results.valid_for: Worst-case bound only. Expected-case impact requires live re-measurement, which is the natural follow-up before plan-lock.

results.label: plan-cycle-handoff
results.description: What the next review-loop:plan cycle for P3-2 should consume from this spike, and what it must additionally produce.

consume_from_spike: Bottleneck identification (skill-internal doc-recon, not prompt size)
Approach (i) trim-prompt is rejected — record this as plan-time pre-decision
must_additionally_produce_in_plan: ONE live re-measurement at current v2.6.31 — pick the cheapest (suggest execute.session-resume) and confirm it reaches Agent dispatch within 180s
Decision among: pure (ii) bump-only / pure (iii) Agent-only stub / hybrid (mix per fixture) / re-scope review-loop.regression assertion set
If hybrid: explicit per-fixture choice locked into ADR (probably ADR-4 or supersede ADR-2)

Implementation: edit setup.timeout_seconds in the relevant smoke fixture .json files, AND edit the per-fixture override `_comment` to flip from `tighten to 1 after BACKLOG follow-up fortifies dispatch within budget` to `min: 1 enforced at <bumped-timeout>s` (or reflect approach (iii) shape if chosen)
Delivery: Commit A (fixture timeouts + override comments + plugin/marketplace.json bump + CHANGELOG); Commit B (BACKLOG.md close-entry citing Commit A SHA, ADR cross-ref)
out_of_scope_for_p3_2: Optimizing SKILL.md doc-recon import surface itself (separate work; affects all sessions, not just smoke fixtures; probably P2-tier when filed)
Refactoring fixture wrap_kind from bash-preseed to direct-claude or vice versa
Adding any new B3 assertion sites — the 8 existing tool_use_events-capturing fixtures are the universe

results.valid_for: Until P3-2 review-loop:plan cycle starts. After plan-lock and ADR write-out, this spike result is consumed and can be considered superseded by the ADR + close-entry SHAs.
